preconditions.text: The CEMS must measure the particular metal and meet the performance specifications for that metal to be used to determine emission compliance with the applicable regulation for that metal.

constraints.text: The diluent (O2) monitor must meet its own performance specifications: 40 CFR part 60, Appendix B, Performance Specification 3.

The multi-metals CEMS must be capable of measuring the total concentrations of two or more of the specified metals (Sb, As, Ba, Be, Cd, Cr, Pb, Hg, Ag, Tl, Mn, Co, Ni, Se) in both their vapor and solid forms.

steps.text: Calibrate, maintain, and operate the CEMS.
Conduct CEMS performance evaluations at other times besides the initial test.

preconditions.text: The Administrator requires a performance evaluation under Section 114 of the Act.

steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Select traverse points to determine the representativeness of the measurement location according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.2 and 2.3.
Relocate the CEMS.

preconditions.text: The cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location, a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established, and the Administrator requires relocation.

constraints.text: The measurement location should be (1) at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other point at which a change of pollutant concentration or flow disturbance may occur and (2) at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust (with equivalent duct diameter calculated as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1).
For Point CEMS, the measurement point should be (1) no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall or (2) within or centrally located over the centroidal area of the stack or duct cross section.

For Path CEMS, the effective measurement path should be (1) totally within the inner area bounded by a line 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall, or (2) have at least 70 percent of the path within the inner 50 percent of the stack or duct cross sectional area, or (3) be centrally located over any part of the centroidal area.

steps.text: Calculate the equivalent duct diameter as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1.
Select traverse measurement point locations according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Sections 2.2 and 2.3.
Displace the RM traverse line up to 30 cm (or 5 percent of the equivalent diameter of the cross section, whichever is less) from the centroidal area.

preconditions.text: The RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements

constraints.text: Measurement location must be at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other flow disturbance, and at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
The RM and CEMS locations should be as close as possible without causing interference with one another.
The CEMS data recorder response range must include zero and a high level value equal to the span value (or support multiple outputs/auto-change if a lower high level value is used).
The RA of the CEMS must be no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data, or 10 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
The CEMS calibration may not drift or deviate from the reference value of the calibration standard by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for each metal.

steps.text: Perform the calibration at a point equal to 80 to 120 percent of the applicable emission standard for each metal.

constraints.text: The CEMS design must allow the determination of calibration drift at the zero level (zero drift) for each metal.

steps.text: Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.

preconditions.text: The affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.

constraints.text: To meet the requirements of Sections 4.3 and 4.4, none of the CD’s or ZD’s may exceed the specification.

steps.text: Conduct a RA test.
Conduct the RA test according to the procedure given in Section 7.

preconditions.text: Determinations must be made following a 24-hour period during which no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place.
The affected facility must be operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in the applicable subpart.

steps.text: Conduct the CD test immediately before the adjustments, or conduct it in such a way that the CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD according to the formula: CD = ((RCEM - RV) / RV) * 100.
Calculate the ZD according to the formula: ZD = ((RCEM - RV) / REM) * 100.

preconditions.text: Periodic automatic or manual adjustments are made to the CEMS zero and calibration settings.

steps.text: Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run (including the exact time of day) in the CEMS data log.
Determine from the CEMS final output (the one used for reporting) the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to the time and duration.

preconditions.text: If iron is chosen, the three levels should be chosen to correspond to those for one of the metals that will be monitored using known sensitivities (documented by the manufacturer) or the CEMS to both metals.

constraints.text: A measure of relative accuracy at a single level is required for each metal measured for compliance purposes by the CEMS.
The concentration of each metal must be detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
The RA must be determined at three levels (0 to 20, 40 to 60, and 80 to 120 percent of the emission limit) for one of the metals which will be monitored, or for iron.

steps.text: Consider system response time, if important, and confirm that the pair of results are on a consistent moisture, temperature, and diluent concentration basis.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.
Reject up to three pairs of measurements if more than nine pairs are obtained, ensuring the total number of measurement pairs used to determine the RA is greater than or equal to nine.
Report all data, including the rejected data.
Use Method 3B, or its approved alternative, as the reference method for diluent (O2) concentration.
Use the manual method for multi-metals in 40 CFR part 266, Appendix IX, Section 3.1 (until superseded by SW-846), or its approved alternative, as the reference method for multi-metals.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values.
Calculate the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets.
Calculate the mean of the differences.
Calculate the standard deviation of each data set and CEMS RA using the equations in Section 8.

preconditions.text: If more than nine pairs of measurements are obtained
Unless otherwise specified in an applicable subpart of the regulations

constraints.text: The total number of measurement pairs used to determine the RA must be greater than or equal to nine.
All data, including the rejected data, must be reported.

steps.text: Spike the appropriate metals in the feed or alter facility operation to raise the metal concentration to a level detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set.

preconditions.text: If metal concentrations are not detectable by both the CEMS and the RM within the bounds of the performance specifications.

steps.text: Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set using Equation 5.
Calculate the Relative Accuracy (RA) using Equation 6.
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Include all data sheets, calculations, and records of CEMS response necessary to substantiate that the performance of the CEMS met the performance specifications.
Report CEMS measurements to the agency.

preconditions.text: Check with the appropriate regional office, State, or local agency for additional reporting requirements.

constraints.text: CEMS measurements must be reported in units of µg/m3 on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.
Use n equal to the number of individual values for t-value lookup.
